title: Kaukenia hexandra
intro: Kaukenia hexandra, also known as Kutu, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of malaria. The bioactive constituents of Kaukenia hexandra include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
